description: Multi-component interaction: Identity allocation race with SelectorCache causes endpoints to get incomplete policy in large Cilium clusters

required_findings.description: Identifies the identity allocator (CachingIdentityAllocator or allocator cache) as the component that allocates security identities and sends events
Identifies the SelectorCache as the component that receives identity updates and provides GetSelections() for policy computation
Identifies endpoint regeneration as the component that computes endpoint policy by calling GetSelections()
Explains that identity events are queued asynchronously via an events channel but SelectorCache may not have processed them before endpoint regeneration starts
Identifies listDone or OnListDone as the signal that marks identity allocation as complete, which was closed too early (before SelectorCache consumed events)
Explains the specific race ordering: UpsertIdentity → event queued → listDone closed → endpoint regeneration → GetSelections() returns incomplete → UpdateIdentities finally processes
Identifies UpdateIdentities as the SelectorCache method that adds identities to cached selectors
Explains that the fix involves blocking/synchronizing listDone until the SelectorCache has processed all events (AllocatorChangeSync event with Done channel)
Mentions that the issue affects large clusters with many identities and selectors because the event queue takes longer to drain
Identifies the identityWatcher or collectEvent as the event processing mechanism that feeds identity changes into the SelectorCache
Mentions that affected endpoints get incorrect eBPF policy maps, causing traffic that should be allowed to be dropped
Identifies the kvstore or CRD list-watch as the source of identity events

file_references.description: Identifies pkg/allocator/cache.go (where OnListDone and listDone channel live)
Identifies pkg/identity/cache/cache.go (where identityWatcher and collectEvent process identity events)
Identifies pkg/identity/cache/allocator.go (where CachingIdentityAllocator and event processing live)
Identifies pkg/allocator/allocator.go (where AllocatorEvent and event types are defined)
Identifies pkg/policy/ directory (SelectorCache, selector resolution, policy computation)
Identifies pkg/endpoint/bpf.go or datapath policy map compilation

causal_chain.description: Component 1 (Identity Allocator): New identities are allocated via kvstore/CRD list-watch. UpsertIdentity creates identity A and sends an IdentityUpdate event to the events channel.
Timing gap: OnListDone closes the listDone channel (signaling allocation is complete) BEFORE the SelectorCache has processed the queued identity events via UpdateIdentities.

Component 2 (Endpoint Regeneration): Endpoint regeneration is triggered (by policy change or new endpoint). The endpoint's policy computation calls GetSelections() on the SelectorCache to find matching identities.

Component 3 (SelectorCache): GetSelections() returns an incomplete set because UpdateIdentities has not yet added identity A to the cached selectors. The endpoint gets a partial/incorrect policy that is compiled into eBPF policy maps.

negative_checks.description: Does NOT blame the eBPF datapath or BPF map compilation as the root cause
Does NOT claim the bug is in the Kubernetes watcher or CNP/CCNP processing logic itself
